methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Exolaunch is a growth-stage space and aerospace company operating within a sector represented by 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace. The breadth of the sector universe is substantial, though direct peer matching at the same stage remains constrained.

Two standard valuation methods could not be applied given available data. Peer-median triangulation failed due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the private set—only 1 adjacent-stage peer was identified against a minimum threshold of 3, making sector-wide medians unreliable. Public-company anchoring via P/S multiples is inapplicable because Exolaunch's revenue is not on file; the Space & Aerospace public median trades at 59.8x revenue across the 6-ticker set, but this multiple cannot be applied without the target's top-line figure.

The absence of both revenue disclosure and a sufficiently dense peer cohort at the same valuation stage means a quantitative range cannot be supported by this analysis. Any valuation of Exolaunch would require either (1) disclosure of annual revenue to enable public-comp anchoring, or (2) identification of additional growth-stage private aerospace companies with disclosed metrics to construct a defensible peer median. Without these inputs, further analytical steps would constitute speculation rather than evidence-based triangulation.
